feed.title: HealthRankings — Health News
feed.subtitle: Latest US health headlines. Full list: https://www.healthrankings.co/healthrankings-news.html

entry.title: Tainted Beef Jerky Caused an Outbreak of Bear Worms
entry.summary: One person was hospitalized with severe symptoms of a Trichinella infection.

entry.title: 11-year-old boy dies of rabies after bat lands on face while he slept
entry.summary: “If you get symptomatic rabies infection, it is near universally fatal. But if you get the prevention before symptoms develop, it is near universally successful.”

entry.title: New colorectal cancer discovery could lead to earlier diagnosis
entry.summary: It’s long been suspected that our gut microbiomes are closely linked to colorectal cancer.

entry.title: People Who Were Stuck In Comas Are Sharing What It Was Actually Like, And I Truly Have No Words
entry.summary: "I was in a medically induced coma for six weeks. There were times I was fully conscious, but I couldn't open my eyes, couldn't move a muscle, and couldn't speak. It was terrifying."

entry.title: She Saved Her Only Birthday Celebration For Her Best Friend—But a Heartless Arrival Left Her Utterly Devastated
entry.summary: The best of the internet, sifted, and sorted.

entry.title: Nutrition apps can help build healthy habits. For some users, their gaming features carry risks
entry.summary: The next time you get a trophy on a smartphone app, it might not be from a game. Gamification has spread into all kinds of online areas. But its spread into nutrition apps has some researchers worried. Health professionals say the apps can be helpful for peop…

entry.title: Scientists discover possible link between 9/11 and accelerated aging
entry.summary: Researchers found that World Trade Center responders with PTSD showed molecular changes associated with faster biological aging and an increased risk of chronic illness.

entry.title: 6 ways to shift your stress mindset to dial down daily anxiety
entry.summary: Stress — and how we think about it — may actually help us thrive.

entry.title: 3 contact lens mistakes that eye doctors wish you would stop making
entry.summary: Contact lenses are safe and effective, but wearing them during some activities can increase the risk of rare but serious complications.

entry.title: 6-Year-Old Girl Bitten by Rabid Bat While Climbing Tree Outside Her Family’s Wisconsin Home
entry.summary: A 6-year-old girl is recovering after being bitten by a rabid bat while climbing a tree outside her family's home in Tigerton, Wisc. During the "terrifying" incident, the animal wrapped its wings around Cece Kale's leg as she screamed for help

entry.title: Don't pressure them: Six science-backed ways to get fussy children to eat vegetables
entry.summary: Small changes in how children experience food everyday can have a lasting impact on what they eat.

entry.title: DR Congo says 1,307 Ebola cases confirmed, including 377 deaths
entry.summary: Outbreak spreads to a fourth province, Haut-Uele, bordering South Sudan and the CAR, according to a media report.

entry.title: Scientists may have finally found how Alzheimer's spreads through the brain
entry.summary: A common brain protein may be giving Alzheimer’s disease an unexpected way to spread, carrying toxic Tau proteins from damaged neurons into healthy ones. By blocking these harmful protein packages before they reach new cells, researchers believe it may one da…

entry.title: Summer Skincare Essentials on Amazon
entry.summary: TMZ may collect a share of sales or other compensation from links on this page.

entry.title: We Asked 4 Heart-Health Experts About the Best Way to Lower Blood Pressure—and They All Said the Same Thing
entry.summary: Heart health experts say following the DASH diet is the most powerful lifestyle change to lower your blood pressure or reduce your hypertension risk.

entry.title: Tejano singer warns of West Nile dangers after mosquito bite leaves him in wheelchair
entry.summary: A single mosquito bite two years ago changed Fernando Silva’s life forever and health officials are now warning that this summer’s record mosquito surge across southeast Texas could put thousands of others at risk.

entry.title: ‘Everyday struggle’: Clark County hairstylist recovering after sudden brain bleed during pregnancy
entry.summary: VANCOUVER, Wash. (KOIN) — A Clark County hairstylist is recovering after a sudden medical emergency during pregnancy left her fighting for her life and requiring emergency brain surgery. Makayla Ferdig says she does not remember when the ordeal began. What sh…

entry.title: Rare contagious cancer is tearing through Washington's Puget Sound clams after East Coast jump
entry.summary: Genetic analysis showed that the Puget Sound cancer belongs to the same lineage previously found in Atlantic Coast soft-shell clams.

entry.title: Cyclosporiasis cases on the rise in Monroe County, health officials say
entry.summary: An outbreak of Cyclosporiasis is spreading in Monroe County, health officials announced on Monday.

entry.title: West Nile virus detected in Davis County mosquitoes
entry.summary: The West Nile virus has been detected in mosquitoes in Davis County.

entry.title: Fish oil supplements no match for Alzheimer's risk, new study finds
entry.summary: A new study from the Keck School of Medicine at USC found a revelation regarding fish oil supplements. Here's the researchers' findings.

entry.title: Too much screentime for young kids will harm their ability to properly learn later, study reveals
entry.summary: Step away from the screens, little ones.

entry.title: Disturbing Case Study Shows Why You Should Never Use a Massage Gun Near Your Eyes
entry.summary: Massage guns are the latest craze in a long history of ideas about vibration therapy that goes back to the ancient Greeks.

entry.title: Propranolol doesn't kill public speaking dread — it just stops your body from becoming a shaky mess
entry.summary: Propranolol doesn’t eliminate mental dread before a talk. It stops your body from dumping adrenaline, steadies your voice, and lets your mind follow. A tech speaker explains.

entry.title: USC scientists just unlocked an endless supply of cancer-fighting immune cells
entry.summary: A new stem-cell-inspired technique allows scientists to grow vast numbers of immune-cell progenitors that can be engineered to hunt cancer and strengthen immune responses. In animal studies, the cells fought tumors, restored immune function, and showed promis…

entry.title: A Yale study found that how you think about getting older may add 7.5 years to your life, more than exercise and more than not smoking
entry.summary: Most people carry an unexamined expectation about ageing. It runs roughly as follows: getting older means getting worse — slower, less capable, less interesting, less relevant. The content varies across individuals, but the basic direction of travel, in most …

entry.title: Rare tick-borne virus turns deadly fast as US cases reach record high, experts warn
entry.summary: Powassan virus, a rare tick-borne illness traced to a 1958 case, has hit record highs in 2025, with 76 U.S. diagnoses and no vaccine available.

entry.title: 6-year-old girl bitten by rabid bat in Wisconsin, family says
entry.summary: A 6-year-old Wisconsin girl is recovering from a rabid bat attack.

entry.title: Cases of Cyclosporiasis confirmed in Monroe County -- here’s what to know
entry.summary: Health officials are investigating a cluster of cyclosporiasis cases among Monroe County residents with illness onsets in the past week.

entry.title: Weight Loss Drugs May Also Be Longevity Drugs, Researchers Say
entry.summary: Weight loss drugs like Ozempic and Zepbound are already changing the way doctors treat obesity and diabetes. Now, researchers are asking a much bigger question: Could these medications also help you live longer? According to The New York Times, early research…

entry.title: Surgeons Warn: Don’t Ignore These Colorectal Cancer Symptoms, No Matter Your Age
entry.summary: Colorectal cancer diagnoses are rising among younger adults, prompting surgeons to highlight subtle warning signs that are often overlooked.

entry.title: 28 Jaw-Dropping Pictures Of The Human Body That Will Completely And Totally Change Your Perspective On Life
entry.summary: My brain is glitching trying to process this all.

entry.title: 3 symptoms men blame on low testosterone — but could be other serious health issues
entry.summary: “Signs and symptoms of low testosterone are often very generalizable and can be due to something else, either in addition to or separately from having low testosterone,” Dr. Justin Dubi…

entry.title: Japan's bold experiment to curb antibiotic misuse has been a huge success. Could it work in the US?
entry.summary: A unique policy in Japan encourages doctors to improve their antibiotic use and thus reduce their contribution to antibiotic resistance. Should the U.S. be taking notes?

entry.title: Doctors thought man had brain cancer — they found live tapeworms instead
entry.summary: Doctors suspected metastatic brain cancer in a 60-year-old man, but scans revealed pork tapeworm larvae causing neurocysticercosis.

entry.title: In October 2024, a team of Chinese researchers at Peking University reported that a 25-year-old woman who had lived with Type 1 diabetes since childhood stopped needing insulin injections within 75 days of receiving an experimental stem cell transplant ge
entry.summary: A 25-year-old woman in northern China spent every day of the preceding decade injecting insulin to keep herself alive — and then, one summer afternoon in 2023, she received the only treatment that has, in the recorded history of Type 1 diabetes therapy, resto…

entry.title: 7-Day High-Protein Anti-Inflammatory Meal Plan for Better Blood Sugar, Created by a Dietitian
entry.summary: Each day provides over 100 grams of protein and 33 grams of fiber to support healthy blood sugar levels in this anti-inflammatory meal plan.

entry.title: Prevalence and severity of mental health problems in early-career researchers: a systematic review and meta-analysis
entry.summary: Mental health concerns among early-career researchers are increasingly gaining attention. This preregistered systematic review and multilevel meta-analysis quantifies psychological distress among early-career researchers, contributing to a better understandin…

entry.title: Light-activated compound kills antibiotic-resistant bacteria by turning its own defense enzyme against it
entry.summary: Antibiotic resistance is becoming an accelerating crisis because of the overuse and misuse of antibiotics over many years. The problem is exacerbated when antibiotics wipe out susceptible bacteria but leave resistant bacteria behind to multiply, further sprea…

entry.title: 14-year-old becomes triple amputee following illness that over 32M Americans had last year
entry.summary: After spending more than 100 days in the hospital, 14-year-old Kaydin Baldwin’s condition quickly became critical.

entry.title: 6 Things That Can Happen When You Start Eating More Protein
entry.summary: Adding more protein to your diet can cause several changes in your body, such as better muscle repair and potential digestive issues.

entry.title: 5-Ingredient Avocado & Black Bean Salad
entry.summary: This Avocado & Black Bean Salad comes together in 20 minutes with a handful of ingredients. The combination of beans and avocado makes this salad satisfying.

entry.title: Man’s Suspected Brain Cancer Turns Out to Be Something Much Creepier
entry.summary: Multiple brain scans were required to find the true culprit behind the man's worsening headaches.

entry.title: DPH confirms state's second measles case, first in a vaccinated adult
entry.summary: The Connecticut Department of Public Health has confirmed the state’s second case of measles in 2026, this time in a vaccinated Hartford County adult…

entry.title: Connecticut reports second measles case of 2026 in vaccinated adult
entry.summary: A second case of measles has been reported in Connecticut, according to officials with the Connecticut Department of Public Health (DPH) on Monday. They said the case was found in a vaccinated adult in Hartford County.

entry.title: A child's living conditions shows up in how their brain functions, WashU scientists find
entry.summary: The results of the study were published earlier this month in the journal Science.

entry.title: Your questions answered on vitamin D, food safety and garden produce
entry.summary: Registered dietitian nutritionist Carrie Dennett dips into her email inbox to answer a few warm-season questions to help you stay healthy and safe.

entry.title: AHA/ACC/ESC/WHF Expert Consensus Document in Collaboration with HFSA, HFA and JHFS: Second Universal Definition of Heart Failure (2026)
entry.summary: In response to the rising global prevalence of heart failure (HF) and limitations in research, surveillance, and prevention, in 2021 multiple cardiac societies convened to issue the First Universal Definition of Heart Failure, establishing uniform terminology…

entry.title: 8 Foods and Drinks That Help Lower Blood Pressure Better Than Taking Supplements
entry.summary: Certain foods and drinks, from leafy greens to beet juice, may help lower blood pressure as part of a heart healthy diet. Here are healthy options to consider.

entry.title: Emerging mRNA vaccine strategies target cancer and pathogenic viruses in potent new ways
entry.summary: The technology that gave the world mRNA COVID vaccines is being tested in a variety of new ways, and emerging research reveals that a crucial T-cell population can be reprogrammed in animal models by reimagining the science that was introduced to the public a…
